templatePlaceholders.THESIS: 不是「不清楚」這麼簡單，而是同一句話、訊號或需求可以合理走向兩種以上解讀。
templatePlaceholders.LEARNING_POSITION: 把 ambiguous 記成「pulled in more than one direction」：問題不是看不見，而是看見後仍可能分岔。
templatePlaceholders.CORE_IDEA: ambiguous describes something open to more than one reasonable interpretation.
templatePlaceholders.CONCEPT_FOCUS: 意義、責任、條件或訊號沒有被釘牢，導致多種解讀同時存在。
templatePlaceholders.TONE_REGISTER: 正式到日常都能用；在需求、規格、法律、設計、語言學與 debug 報告中特別有用。
templatePlaceholders.USE_WARNING: 不要把 ambiguous 和 vague 混在一起；vague 是輪廓模糊，ambiguous 是可能有多個明確方向。
templatePlaceholders.SHORT_DEFINITION: Able to be understood in more than one way, often because meaning, scope, or intent is not fixed enough.
templatePlaceholders.CONFUSION_NOTE: vague 是不夠具體；ambiguous 是有兩種以上可能解釋。
templatePlaceholders.ORIGIN_PARAGRAPH: ambiguous comes from Latin elements meaning around and to drive or move. The old picture is meaning being driven in more than one direction.
templatePlaceholders.ORIGIN_MEMORY: 你可以把它想成一個路牌站在岔路口：它不是完全空白，而是讓兩條路都看起來合理。
templatePlaceholders.MEMORY_HOOK: Ambiguous requirements are forks disguised as sentences.
templatePlaceholders.MEMORY_EXPLANATION: 一句規格如果可以被 firmware、QA、PM 各自解讀成不同實作，它就不是單純寫得短，而是留下了分岔點。
templatePlaceholders.DAILY_USAGE: The message was ambiguous, so I could not tell whether the meeting was canceled or just moved.
templatePlaceholders.PROFESSIONAL_USAGE: An ambiguous requirement can pass review quietly and become an expensive bug later.
templatePlaceholders.DOMAIN_USAGE: In parsing, ambiguous grammar allows the same input to produce more than one valid structure.
templatePlaceholders.COLLOCATION_NOTE: Ambiguous 常搭配 statement、requirement、signal、result，指解讀上的分岔。
templatePlaceholders.COLLOCATION_NOTE_1: 指規格可以被不同人合理解讀成不同實作。
templatePlaceholders.COLLOCATION_NOTE_2: 指觀察到的訊號不足以唯一指向某個原因。
templatePlaceholders.COLLOCATION_NOTE_3: 指文字本身留下多重解釋空間。
templatePlaceholders.NEIGHBOR_SELF: 同一材料可導向多個合理解讀
templatePlaceholders.NEIGHBOR_SELF_USE: 句子、規格、訊號或責任界線會分岔時
templatePlaceholders.NEIGHBOR_1_MEANING: 輪廓不清、缺少具體資訊
templatePlaceholders.NEIGHBOR_1_USE: 當問題是太籠統，而不是可被清楚地往兩邊解讀
templatePlaceholders.MODERN_USE_1: Modern English uses ambiguous to diagnose language, requirements, data, laws, images, and behavior that can support more than one interpretation.
templatePlaceholders.MODERN_USE_2: In engineering, calling something ambiguous is not just criticism; it is a request to add a condition, threshold, owner, or example.
templatePlaceholders.DICTIONARY_SOURCE_NOTE: Dictionary definitions support the sense of having more than one possible meaning or interpretation.
templatePlaceholders.ETYMOLOGY_SOURCE_NOTE: Etymology notes connect the word to Latin elements for around and driving or moving.
templatePlaceholders.MODERN_SOURCE_NOTE: Modern examples support use with wording, statements, evidence, signals, and situations.
templatePlaceholders.CHECK_MEANING: 我能說出 ambiguous 和 vague 的差異。
templatePlaceholders.CHECK_ORIGIN: 我記得它有 meaning driven in multiple directions 的畫面。
templatePlaceholders.CHECK_SENTENCE: 我能用它造一句需求、訊號或訊息解讀情境的英文句子。
templatePlaceholders.PRACTICE_SENTENCE: The reset requirement is ambiguous because it never defines the timeout window.

indexEntry.thesis: 不是「不清楚」這麼簡單，而是同一句話、訊號或需求可以合理走向兩種以上解讀。

checks.label: 說出 ambiguous 和 vague 的差異
記得它有往多個方向被推動的畫面
用它造一句需求、訊號或訊息解讀情境的英文句子
